label: Dengue
description: Mosquito-borne viral infection endemic to tropical and subtropical regions worldwide.

label: Malaria
description: Parasitic disease transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes, causing fever and organ damage.

label: Chagas Disease
description: Parasitic illness caused by Trypanosoma cruzi, spread by triatomine bugs.

label: Leprosy
description: Chronic bacterial infection affecting skin, nerves, and mucous membranes.

label: Buruli Ulcer
description: Necrotizing skin infection caused by Mycobacterium ulcerans, mainly in West Africa.

label: Schistosomiasis
description: Parasitic disease caused by blood flukes acquired through contaminated freshwater.

label: Leishmaniasis
description: Parasitic disease spread by sandfly bites, causing skin or visceral organ damage.

label: Sleeping Sickness
description: Tsetse fly-transmitted parasitic disease affecting the central nervous system.

label: River Blindness
description: Parasitic disease caused by Onchocerca volvulus, leading to blindness.

label: Lymphatic Filariasis
description: Mosquito-transmitted parasitic disease causing severe lymphatic system swelling.

label: Trachoma
description: Bacterial eye infection and leading infectious cause of preventable blindness.

label: Guinea Worm
description: Parasitic infection nearing eradication, contracted through contaminated water.

label: Rabies
description: Fatal viral disease transmitted through animal bites, nearly 100% lethal once symptomatic.

label: Yaws
description: Chronic bacterial skin infection caused by Treponema pallidum pertenue.

label: Chikungunya
description: Mosquito-borne viral disease causing fever and severe joint pain.

label: Leptospirosis
description: Bacterial disease spread through animal urine-contaminated water or soil.

label: Mycetoma
description: Chronic subcutaneous fungal or bacterial infection affecting limbs.

label: Echinococcosis
description: Parasitic cyst-forming disease caused by Echinococcus tapeworms.

label: Fascioliasis
description: Liver fluke infection acquired from contaminated aquatic plants or water.

label: Soil-transmitted Helminths
description: Intestinal worm infections affecting over a billion people in developing regions.

label: Breast Cancer
description: Most commonly diagnosed cancer worldwide, arising from breast tissue cells.

label: Lung Cancer
description: Leading cause of cancer death, strongly linked to smoking and air pollution.

label: Colon Cancer
description: Cancer of the large intestine, highly treatable when detected early via screening.

label: Pancreatic Cancer
description: Aggressive cancer with low survival rates due to late-stage diagnosis.

label: Leukemia
description: Blood cancer affecting white blood cells in bone marrow and lymphatic system.

label: Lymphoma
description: Cancer of the lymphatic system including Hodgkin and non-Hodgkin types.

label: Prostate Cancer
description: Most common cancer in men, often slow-growing but variable in aggressiveness.

label: Ovarian Cancer
description: Gynecological cancer often diagnosed at advanced stages with poor prognosis.

label: Melanoma
description: Deadliest form of skin cancer, strongly associated with UV radiation exposure.

label: Liver Cancer
description: Cancer closely linked to hepatitis infections and chronic liver disease.

label: Stomach Cancer
description: Gastric cancer associated with H. pylori infection and dietary factors.

label: Cervical Cancer
description: HPV-linked cancer of the cervix, largely preventable through vaccination.

label: Bladder Cancer
description: Cancer of the urinary bladder, linked to smoking and chemical exposures.

label: Brain Cancer
description: Primary tumors of the brain and central nervous system with poor survival.

label: Kidney Cancer
description: Renal cell carcinoma accounting for most adult kidney cancers.

label: Thyroid Cancer
description: Endocrine cancer with rising incidence but generally favorable prognosis.

label: Esophageal Cancer
description: Cancer of the esophagus with high mortality and regional variation.

label: Multiple Myeloma
description: Blood cancer of plasma cells in bone marrow causing bone lesions.

label: Heart Disease
description: Leading global cause of death encompassing coronary and ischemic conditions.

label: Stroke
description: Sudden interruption of blood supply to the brain causing cell death.

label: Hypertension
description: Chronically elevated blood pressure and major risk factor for heart disease.

label: Atherosclerosis
description: Buildup of plaque in artery walls restricting blood flow to organs.

label: Heart Failure
description: Chronic condition where the heart cannot pump blood effectively.

label: Arrhythmia
description: Irregular heart rhythms including atrial fibrillation and tachycardia.

label: Peripheral Artery Disease
description: Narrowing of peripheral arteries reducing blood flow to the limbs.

label: Aortic Aneurysm
description: Dangerous widening of the aorta that may rupture without warning.

label: Cardiomyopathy
description: Disease of the heart muscle affecting its ability to pump blood.

label: Deep Vein Thrombosis
description: Blood clot formation in deep veins, usually in the legs, risking embolism.

label: Pulmonary Embolism
description: Blockage of a pulmonary artery by a blood clot from a deep vein.

label: Rheumatic Heart Disease
description: Heart valve damage from rheumatic fever, prevalent in low-income countries.

label: Endocarditis
description: Infection of the heart's inner lining, typically affecting heart valves.

label: Myocarditis
description: Inflammation of the heart muscle often caused by viral infections.

label: Alzheimer's Disease
description: Progressive neurodegenerative disorder and leading cause of dementia.

label: Parkinson's Disease
description: Degenerative movement disorder caused by loss of dopamine-producing neurons.

label: Epilepsy
description: Neurological disorder marked by recurrent unprovoked seizures.

label: Multiple Sclerosis
description: Autoimmune disease damaging nerve cell insulation in the brain and spine.

label: ALS
description: Fatal motor neuron disease causing progressive muscle paralysis.

label: Huntington's Disease
description: Inherited neurodegenerative disorder causing progressive motor and cognitive decline.

label: Migraine
description: Recurring severe headache disorder often with sensory disturbances.

label: Neuropathy
description: Damage or dysfunction of peripheral nerves causing pain and numbness.

label: Meningitis
description: Inflammation of brain and spinal cord membranes from infection.

label: Cerebral Palsy
description: Group of permanent movement disorders appearing in early childhood.

label: Spinal Cord Injury
description: Damage to the spinal cord causing loss of muscle function and sensation.

label: Traumatic Brain Injury
description: Brain damage caused by external mechanical force or impact to the head.

label: Narcolepsy
description: Chronic sleep disorder causing overwhelming daytime drowsiness.

label: Essential Tremor
description: Common movement disorder causing involuntary rhythmic shaking.

label: Asthma
description: Chronic airway inflammation causing reversible breathing difficulty.

label: COPD
description: Progressive lung disease causing obstructed airflow and breathing difficulty.

label: Pneumonia
description: Lung infection causing inflammation of the air sacs, a major killer globally.

label: Tuberculosis
description: Bacterial lung infection and one of the deadliest infectious diseases worldwide.

label: Lung Fibrosis
description: Progressive scarring of lung tissue leading to respiratory failure.

label: Sleep Apnea
description: Disorder causing repeated breathing interruptions during sleep.

label: Bronchiectasis
description: Permanent widening of the bronchi causing chronic cough and infections.

label: Sarcoidosis
description: Inflammatory disease forming granulomas in lungs and other organs.

label: Pulmonary Hypertension
description: High blood pressure in the pulmonary arteries straining the right heart.

label: Pleural Effusion
description: Abnormal fluid buildup between the lung and chest wall lining.

label: Acute Respiratory Distress
description: Life-threatening lung condition causing severe shortness of breath.

label: Pertussis
description: Highly contagious bacterial respiratory infection known as whooping cough.

label: Lupus
description: Systemic autoimmune disease where the body attacks its own tissues.

label: Rheumatoid Arthritis
description: Chronic inflammatory disorder affecting the joints and connective tissue.

label: Crohn's Disease
description: Inflammatory bowel disease causing chronic digestive tract inflammation.

label: Celiac Disease
description: Autoimmune condition triggered by gluten ingestion damaging the small intestine.

label: Type 1 Diabetes
description: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas.

label: Psoriasis
description: Chronic skin disease causing rapid cell buildup forming itchy dry patches.

label: Ulcerative Colitis
description: Inflammatory bowel disease causing chronic inflammation of the colon lining.

label: Sjögren's Syndrome
description: Autoimmune disorder attacking moisture-producing glands causing dryness.

label: Vasculitis
description: Inflammation of blood vessels that can restrict blood flow to organs.

label: Scleroderma
description: Autoimmune connective tissue disease causing skin and organ hardening.

label: Myasthenia Gravis
description: Autoimmune disorder causing weakness in voluntary skeletal muscles.

label: Ankylosing Spondylitis
description: Chronic spinal arthritis that can cause vertebrae to fuse together.

label: Type 2 Diabetes
description: Metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance and high blood sugar.

label: Obesity
description: Excessive body fat accumulation increasing risk of numerous diseases.

label: Gout
description: Inflammatory arthritis from uric acid crystal deposits in joints.

label: NAFLD
description: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease linked to obesity and metabolic syndrome.

label: Metabolic Syndrome
description: Cluster of conditions increasing risk of heart disease and diabetes.

label: Hyperlipidemia
description: Abnormally elevated blood lipid levels increasing cardiovascular risk.

label: Hypothyroidism
description: Underactive thyroid gland leading to slowed metabolism and fatigue.

label: Hyperthyroidism
description: Overactive thyroid gland causing accelerated metabolism and weight loss.

label: Osteoporosis
description: Progressive bone density loss increasing fracture risk in older adults.

label: Phenylketonuria
description: Inherited metabolic disorder preventing proper phenylalanine breakdown.

label: Porphyria
description: Group of disorders caused by abnormalities in heme production pathways.

label: Wilson's Disease
description: Genetic disorder causing toxic copper accumulation in liver and brain.

label: HIV/AIDS
description: Viral infection attacking the immune system, progressing to AIDS if untreated.

label: COVID-19
description: Respiratory illness caused by SARS-CoV-2 that triggered a global pandemic.

label: Hepatitis B
description: Viral liver infection transmitted through blood and body fluids.

label: Hepatitis C
description: Blood-borne viral infection causing chronic liver disease and cirrhosis.

label: Influenza
description: Highly contagious respiratory virus causing seasonal epidemics worldwide.

label: Measles
description: Highly contagious viral disease preventable by vaccination.

label: Ebola
description: Severe hemorrhagic fever virus with high fatality rates in outbreaks.

label: Cholera
description: Acute diarrheal illness caused by Vibrio cholerae contaminating water.

label: Typhoid
description: Bacterial infection from contaminated food or water causing sustained fever.

label: Plague (Yersinia pestis)
description: Zoonotic bacterial infection caused by Yersinia pestis, transmitted via flea bites or respiratory droplets. Manifests as bubonic, septicemic, or pneumonic plague. Endemic in parts of Africa, Asia, and the Americas with sporadic outbreaks.

label: MRSA
description: Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus causing hard-to-treat infections.

label: C. difficile
description: Bacterial infection causing severe diarrhea, often hospital-acquired.

label: Zika
description: Mosquito-borne virus linked to birth defects including microcephaly.

label: West Nile Virus
description: Mosquito-transmitted virus that can cause serious neurological disease.

label: Norovirus
description: Highly contagious virus causing acute gastroenteritis outbreaks worldwide.

label: HPV
description: Human papillomavirus, the most common sexually transmitted infection.

label: Cystic Fibrosis
description: Inherited disorder causing severe damage to lungs and digestive system.

label: Sickle Cell Disease
description: Inherited blood disorder producing abnormally shaped red blood cells.

label: Down Syndrome
description: Chromosomal condition caused by trisomy 21 affecting development.

label: Hemophilia
description: Inherited bleeding disorder caused by deficient blood clotting factors.

label: Thalassemia
description: Inherited blood disorder reducing hemoglobin production.

label: Marfan Syndrome
description: Connective tissue disorder affecting the heart, eyes, and skeleton.

label: Turner Syndrome
description: Chromosomal condition affecting females with partial or missing X chromosome.

label: Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy
description: Severe progressive muscle degeneration caused by dystrophin gene mutations.

label: Fragile X Syndrome
description: Most common inherited cause of intellectual disability and autism.

label: Tay-Sachs Disease
description: Fatal genetic disorder destroying nerve cells in the brain and spinal cord.

label: Gaucher Disease
description: Lysosomal storage disorder causing organ enlargement and bone damage.

label: Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome
description: Connective tissue disorders causing hypermobile joints and fragile skin.

label: Depression
description: Mood disorder causing persistent feelings of sadness and loss of interest.

label: Schizophrenia
description: Severe mental disorder affecting thinking, perception, and behavior.

label: PTSD
description: Trauma-induced disorder causing flashbacks, nightmares, and severe anxiety.

label: Anxiety Disorders
description: Group of mental disorders characterized by excessive worry and fear.

label: Bipolar Disorder
description: Mood disorder causing extreme emotional highs and depressive lows.

label: OCD
description: Disorder featuring unwanted repetitive thoughts and compulsive behaviors.

label: ADHD
description: Neurodevelopmental disorder affecting attention, impulsivity, and activity levels.

label: Autism Spectrum Disorder
description: Neurodevelopmental condition affecting social communication and behavior.

label: Anorexia Nervosa
description: Eating disorder characterized by extreme food restriction and weight loss.

label: Substance Use Disorder
description: Chronic condition involving compulsive use of substances despite harmful effects.

label: Borderline Personality Disorder
description: Disorder marked by unstable moods, relationships, and self-image.

label: Insomnia
description: Persistent difficulty falling or staying asleep despite adequate opportunity.

label: Endometrial Cancer
description: Cancer of the uterine lining, the most common gynecological malignancy.

label: Congenital Heart Defects
description: Structural heart abnormalities present at birth affecting blood flow.

label: Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever
description: Severe complication of dengue infection causing plasma leakage and bleeding.

label: Sepsis
description: Life-threatening organ dysfunction caused by the body's response to infection.

label: Prion Disease
description: Fatal neurodegenerative disorders caused by misfolded prion proteins.

label: Polycystic Kidney Disease
description: Inherited disorder causing fluid-filled cysts to grow in the kidneys.

label: Chronic Kidney Disease
description: Progressive loss of kidney function often caused by diabetes and hypertension, requiring dialysis or transplantation in advanced stages.

label: Cirrhosis
description: End-stage scarring of the liver caused by chronic hepatitis, alcohol use, or fatty liver disease, leading to liver failure.

label: Preeclampsia
description: Dangerous pregnancy complication characterized by high blood pressure and organ damage, a leading cause of maternal and fetal mortality worldwide.

label: Endometriosis
description: Chronic inflammatory condition where tissue similar to the uterine lining grows outside the uterus, causing severe pain and infertility, affecting 1 in 10 women.

label: Tetanus
description: Acute bacterial infection caused by Clostridium tetani spores entering wounds, producing a neurotoxin that causes severe muscle spasms and lockjaw, preventable by vaccination.

label: Rotavirus
description: Leading cause of severe diarrheal disease in children under five worldwide, killing 128,000 children annually despite availability of effective vaccines.
